items.text: Sustainability We continue to evolve our approach to sustainability.
items.rationale: Generic framing statement about evolving sustainability approach with no specific content.

items.text: In 2026, we updated our prior circularity and climate ambitions.
items.rationale: Vague statement that goals were updated without specifics; framing language.

items.text: Production and marketing includes joint venture production we market plus our pro rata share of the remaining production produced and marketed by the joint venture, and production via third-party tolling arrangements.
items.rationale: Definitional clarification of terminology, not a risk.

items.text: Sustainability Actions in 2025 .
items.rationale: Section heading with no substantive content.

items.text: We also completed the shutdown of refining operations at our Houston refinery in the first quarter of 2025.
items.rationale: Adds a specific factual detail (completed Houston refinery shutdown) that elaborates on an existing operational risk theme.

items.text: Additionally, we advocate for enabling policy support and government-backed frameworks to progress these initiatives.
items.rationale: Generic advocacy statement about policy support, no specific risk.

items.text: Our ambition to produce and market 800 thousand metric tons of recycled and renewable-based polymers annually by 2030 will require the successful completion of our MoReTec -1 plant, expansion of our recycling footprint through inorganic growth, increased demand for circular products and supportive regulatory frameworks.
items.rationale: Elaborates on dependencies and conditions tied to an existing sustainability ambition, adding specificity to prior risk.

items.text: Risk Factors for additional risks associated with each of our ambitions.
items.rationale: Cross-reference to another section, not substantive content.

items.text: While we continue to invest in our MoReTec- 1 plant as planned, we are delaying certain sustainability-related capital projects to preserve capital during the cycle downturn.
items.rationale: Discloses a new specific action of delaying capital projects due to cycle downturn, a material business risk.

items.text: Stepping up Performance and Culture —As part of our culture transformation, in 2025, we prioritized employee feedback and reinforced cultural attributes aligned with our strategy.
items.rationale: Generic culture initiative narrative, not a risk.

items.text: One key initiative is the culture ambassador program, where employees serve as advocates for culture change and promote global understanding.
items.rationale: Describes a culture program with no risk implication.

items.text: Ambassadors receive context, training, and information on company initiatives to support engagement and share feedback with leadership.
items.rationale: Describes ambassador training, no risk content.

items.text: Insights from this program, along with our 2025 engagement survey, inform leadership actions to foster an environment that drives engagement, innovation and business results.
items.rationale: Describes survey insights informing leadership, no risk.

items.text: Demographics —As of December 31, 2025, we had approximately 18,970 employees, a decrease of approximately 7% when compared to 2024, driven by fixed cost reductions attributable to our cash improvement plan and Houston Refinery shutdown.
items.rationale: Specific quantitative disclosure of 7% workforce reduction tied to cost cutting and refinery shutdown, indicating operational restructuring risk.

items.text: In 2025, our networks continued to grow, and 24% of our employees are members of a network.
items.rationale: Generic statistic about employee network membership, no risk.

items.text: Survey results also indicate members are more likely to participate in surveys and have a stronger strategic alignment to our organization compared to non-members.
items.rationale: Survey engagement statistic, no risk.

items.text: In 2025, we expanded access to our LYB University offerings by moving several of our formal programs and courses to open/self-enrollment, which allowed a greater number of employees the opportunity to enroll.
items.rationale: Describes training program changes, no risk.

items.text: Over 500 employees participated in our global leadership development programs, for people leaders and those who lead through influence.
items.rationale: Participation statistic in leadership program, no risk.

items.text: In addition, we offered a new series that focused on preparing people leaders to lead change and navigate through uncertainty and disruption, with 138 leaders in attendance.
items.rationale: Describes a new training series, no risk.

items.text: We are planning to continue this series into 2026.
items.rationale: Statement of future plan to continue a program, no risk.

items.text: Senior Vice President, Olefins & Polyolefins Americas and Refining from April 2024 to February 2025.
items.rationale: Executive biography detail, not a risk.

items.text: Vice President, Finance and Strategy, Intermediates & Derivatives from November 2022 to April 2024.
items.rationale: Executive biography detail, not a risk.

items.text: 15 Table of Contents Name and Age Significant Experience Yvonne van der Laan, 54 Executive Vice President, Circular and Low Carbon Solutions since October 2022.
items.rationale: Executive biography detail, not a risk.

items.text: We are currently constructing our first commercial-scale chemical recycling facility using our MoReTec technology, located at our site in Wesseling, Germany.
items.rationale: Factual statement about facility construction location, contextual not risk itself.

items.text: Building a commercial-scale facility utilizing a new technology can face technical and other challenges resulting in increased costs.
items.rationale: Identifies a specific technical/cost risk associated with building a new commercial-scale facility using new technology.

items.text: In 2025, we announced the deferral of construction on our Flex-2 project in Channelview to preserve capital during the market downturn and also postponed the final investment decision on certain projects, such as MoReTec -2, which could result in increased costs.
items.rationale: Discloses specific project deferrals (Flex-2, MoReTec-2) that could increase costs, a concrete new business risk.

items.text: For example, in the third quarter of 2025, a prolonged downturn in, and outlook for, the European petrochemical and global automotive industries, combined with the sustained decline in our market capitalization, resulted in non-cash impairment charges of $1,182 million, presented in both Goodwill impairments and Other impairments on the Consolidated Statements of Income (Loss).
items.rationale: Discloses a specific $1,182 million impairment charge tied to market downturn, a material financial risk event.

items.text: For example, in March 2025, we announced the permanent closure of the PO/SM production unit at the Maasvlakte site in the Netherlands, a joint venture between us and Covestro, resulting in the recognition of $126 million in shutdown costs during the year ended December 31, 2025.
items.rationale: Discloses specific plant closure and $126 million shutdown cost, a new material financial/operational event.

items.text: In 2025, we agreed to sell certain European olefins and polyolefins assets and the associated business.
items.rationale: Announces a new divestiture of assets, introducing a new business risk regarding portfolio changes.

items.text: The sites to be sold are located in Berre l’Etang (France), Münchsmünster (Germany), Carrington (United Kingdom), and Tarragona (Spain), and closing is expected in the second quarter of 2026.
items.rationale: Provides additional specific details (locations, timing) about the divestiture risk already introduced.

items.text: There can be no assurance that announced dispositions - including our European divestiture - will be successfully completed on the expected timeline or at all, and transactions that are delayed or abandoned may cause additional disruption to the business.
items.rationale: Adds risk language about uncertainty of completing announced dispositions, expanding on the divestiture risk.

items.text: Despite recent government actions to delay or decrease regulatory obligations in certain jurisdictions, we generally expect that these requirements may become more stringent over the longer term.
items.rationale: Restates existing regulatory risk expectation with updated context about government delays, same underlying risk.

items.text: In addition, it remains uncertain whether the EU will implement a carbon border adjustment mechanism for organic chemicals and polymers, and, if so, how such a mechanism would affect the competitiveness of our products and our exposure to higher carbon costs.
items.rationale: Introduces specific uncertainty about EU carbon border adjustment mechanism and its competitive/cost impact.

items.text: While the negotiations ended in 2025 without reaching an agreement, they demonstrated significant interest globally in addressing these issues.
items.rationale: Adds updated factual context about failed negotiations, expanding on existing regulatory/political risk discussion.

items.text: In 2026, we updated these goals.
items.rationale: Duplicate/rephrased statement that goals were updated, same as sentence about 2026 update.

items.text: We may need to further update our goals to address market changes.
items.rationale: Generic cautionary statement about possibly updating goals in future, no specific new risk.

items.text: We have set a 2030 circularity goal for producing and marketing recycled and renewable-based polymers annually by 2030.
items.rationale: Restates existing circularity goal already referenced elsewhere, no new specifics.
